w:r.w:t: The definitive build brief — proof architecture, homepage wireframe, full sitemap, copy direction
Version 1.0 | June 2026 | Prepared for Alom + Fabrizio

This is the complete build brief for calonaisolutions.com V2. It incorporates everything from the audit, rebuild brief, and optimisation brief — plus the proof intelligence extracted from the Volt Secure, HeatGlow, The Cwtch, and BRIC Living proposals.
You do not need any other document to build the site. This is the single source of truth.
1. The Positioning Statement — One Page, Non-Negotiable
2. The Proof Architecture — What We Have and How to Use It
3. The Operational Visibility Framework — Final Spec
8. Proof / Case Studies Page — Full Structure
14. What to Delete from the Current Site
16. The Three Decisions for Today's Alom Sync
NON-NEGOTIABLE. EVERY PAGE, EVERY HEADLINE, EVERY WORD IS TESTED AGAINST THIS.
Calon builds operational systems for founder-led service businesses.

We identify where margin is leaking — through slow quoting, poor job handover, untracked overruns, and missed repeat work — and we build the systems that stop it.
We are not an AI agency. We are not a chatbot company. We are not a no-code automation shop.
We build things: quoting systems, job handover workflows, scheduling tools, timesheet verification platforms, booking control systems, reporting dashboards, and the operational infrastructure that growing service businesses don't have time to build themselves.
THE POSITIONING TEST — APPLY BEFORE PUBLISHING ANYTHING
A security company founder lands on the site and thinks: 'AI chatbot agency. Not for me.'
Or: 'This is a consulting firm that will map my processes and leave.'
A security company founder lands on the site and thinks: 'These people understand exactly how my business operates.'
And: 'I want to know what they would find in my business.'
WHAT WE HAVE FROM THE PROPOSALS — AND HOW TO USE IT ETHICALLY

After reviewing all five proposals, Calon has more proof than it thinks it has. The question is not 'do we have proof?' — it is 'how do we present diagnosed opportunities vs delivered outcomes honestly without overstating results?'
Present proof in three tiers. Be explicit about which tier each piece belongs to. Do not conflate them.
Systems built, handed over, in use. Real operational outcomes measured. Example: Volt Secure timesheet platform, The Cwtch booking system.
Present as case studies. Name the sector and business type. Show before/after metrics. Use real quotes where available. This is the gold standard proof.

Discovery completed. Problems identified and quantified in £. Proposal produced. Client has the diagnostic output whether or not they proceeded. Example: HeatGlow (£14.4k–£33.6k identified), Volt Secure timesheet (£20,800/yr identified).

Present as 'Operational Diagnostics'. Label clearly: 'Opportunity identified through diagnostic process.' Do NOT imply the system is live if it isn't. Show the diagnostic methodology and the £ finding.
Proposal produced based on identified needs. No diagnostic session completed. Example: BRIC Living rewards platform, KPA Property.

Present as 'Systems Designed'. Show the scope and the commercial logic. Do not attach outcome figures unless the system is live. Use as proof of sector depth and commercial thinking, not operational results.
Every proof asset available right now, tiered correctly:
Full case study — Security & FM page, Proof page, homepage testimonial
Supporting proof — shows commercial framing capability
Case study — Hospitality section, Proof page
Diagnostic story — Trades page. Label as 'opportunity identified'. Do not claim delivered.
Systems Designed — secondary proof only. Good for showing commercial model depth.
2.3 The Volt Secure Case Study — The Flagship Proof Asset
Volt Secure is the most commercially powerful proof asset Calon has. It covers two separate delivered systems, both with hard £ figures attached. This becomes the primary case study on the site.
Volt Secure Case Study — Headline Numbers

SECURITY & ELECTRICAL | 10 engineers | South Wales
System 1 — AI Quotation Platform Problem: Manual quoting taking 24 hours per proposal. FM advisory quotes (120 job sheets/week) processed manually by one person. Built: AI quotation system with RAG, FM advisory auto-draft, proposal tracking, digital signature. Result: 7-page proposal reduced from 24 hours to under 10 minutes. Zero advisory quotes missed.
System 2 — Timesheet Verification Problem: £20,800/year in timesheet overpayments across 10 engineers. Built: RAM Tracking API integration, automatic GPS cross-reference, discrepancy dashboard, Xero export. Result: £15,200 net year-1 saving. Payroll confrontation eliminated. Engineers self-corrected once system went live.
Total investment: £10,000 + £300/month Net year-1 benefit: £15,200+ (timesheet saving alone, excluding quoting ROI)
2.4 How to Present Proof Ethically When You Don't Have 20 Completed Builds
The honest framing that still converts:

Do not claim 20 completed transformations when you have 3. Do claim 3 delivered systems with specificity that makes them more credible than 20 vague testimonials.
The site should use this exact language in the proof section header:
'We have completed [X] operational builds. Every system on this page is real, built, and in use today. Where we show a diagnostic finding rather than a delivered outcome, we say so.'
This is not a weakness — it is a trust signal. It tells the founder that when you claim something, it is true. Most agencies claiming '100+ transformations' on their site have nothing behind it.
CALON'S SIGNATURE IP — BUILD THIS GRAPHIC FIRST

This is the most important visual asset on the entire site. It becomes the spine of the homepage, the diagnostic methodology, the proposal structure, and the LinkedIn content strategy. Build the SVG before writing any other code.
Six stages. Each stage has: a name, a leak type, a typical £ leak per year for a £1m–£2m service business, and a Calon fix. The graphic runs left to right on desktop, stacks vertically on mobile.
Slow response. Leads go cold. Multi-channel chaos.
Manual build. 24hr+ turnaround. No follow-up logic.
AI-assisted quoting. Pre-filled history. Tracked opens.
Phone/WhatsApp chaos. Conflicts. Wrong engineer assigned.
Scheduling board. Job type matching. Automated reminders.
Engineer arrives without scope. 30min clarification. Overruns not captured.
Job handover doc. Scope pre-confirmed. Overrun flag.
Extras done, not charged. Timesheet inflation. Slow close.
Time tracking. Timesheet verification. Auto-invoice trigger.
Relationship-led. No systematic trigger. Maintenance missed.
Repeat work scheduler. Proactive reminders. Service tracking.
Total typical leak — £79,000–£137,000/year on a £1m–£2m service business.

None of this shows on the P&L. All of it hides in the workflow.
Below the framework on the site: 'The diagnostic maps every one of these stages. In 45 minutes, we tell you which leak is costing you the most.'
Build as an SVG or CSS-based horizontal flow chart in Webflow
Stage boxes: dark navy background (#0A1628), stage name in white, number in teal
Leak row: dark red background, leak text in pale red
£ row: amber background, value in dark navy bold
Arrows connecting each stage — simple right-pointing chevrons in teal
On mobile: stack vertically, each stage becomes a full-width card
On desktop: all six fit in a single row across the full content width
This graphic appears: homepage hero area, How It Works page, booking page (simplified version)
It also becomes the slide 1 of every proposal and the anchor of every discovery conversation
Convert the broadest ICP. Every founder-led service business in the target sectors feels seen in the first 5 seconds.
Outbound destination for trades cold email/BNI. Pain-specific. Proof-specific.
Outbound destination for security/FM outreach. Pain-specific.
Risk reduction. Answers 'what actually happens if I book?' Shows the build, not just the diagnostic.
Full case study library. Tiered proof (Delivered / Diagnosed / Designed). Sector-tagged.
Named founders. Why Calon exists. What we don't take on. Geographic credibility.
Pure conversion. Calendar embed. No nav. No distractions. One action.
Useful error page — link back to homepage and booking page.
What is NOT in the sitemap

No blog. No AI services page. No chatbot page. No industry sub-pages beyond Trades and Security/FM. No case study sub-pages initially — the proof page handles everything until there is enough content to warrant individual pages. These get added in V3, not now.
THIS IS THE MOST IMPORTANT PAGE. BUILD IT EXACTLY AS SPECIFIED.

FOR TRADES · SECURITY · FM · FIELD SERVICE
Styling: pill/chip element, dark navy background, teal dot on left, uppercase spaced text. This appears before the headline so the ICP is established before a word is read.

Most service businesses don't have a sales problem. They have an operational one.
Styling: 'operational' is italicised and in teal (#0EA5E9). Everything else is white. Large — 56–64px on desktop. The single best line on the site.

Most £500k–£5m service businesses are losing £60,000–£100,000 a year in recoverable margin. It doesn't show on the P&L. It hides in the workflow. We find it and build the systems that stop it.
Styling: 20–22px. Grey-white (#CBD5E1). One paragraph.

PRIMARY: [Book the Diagnostic →] — teal background, dark text, full-width on mobile SECONDARY: [See how it works] — ghost/outline button
No email capture. No form. Both buttons go to named pages. Primary → /diagnostic. Secondary → /how-it-works.

THREE STATS:
£82k avg. margin recovered per engagement 14wk average diagnostic to full system handover 3 delivered operational systems, in use today
Styling: three columns, monospace or code-style numbers in teal. Below each number: label in small grey caps.
ATTRIBUTION LINE (required): 'Based on completed client engagements, 2024–2026. Anonymised.'
NOTE: Replace '23 founder-led firms diagnosed' with '3 delivered operational systems' — this is honest and more impressive because it is specific. Add the diagnosed figure separately if needed.
Hero visual — RIGHT SIDE on desktop, BELOW copy on mobile

The Operational Visibility Framework graphic (Section 3).
A simplified version: six stage boxes with red leak markers. The £ values appear as a tooltip or on hover on desktop. Static on mobile.
This makes the abstract value proposition immediately concrete before the founder has scrolled anywhere.
01 | THE DIAGNOSIS Styling: small caps, teal dot, section counter

Margin leaks at every stage of the job. You won't find it on the P&L. It hides in the workflow.
Styling: 'leaks' in teal italics. White text. 40–48px.
Three stage cards. BEFORE / DURING / AFTER the job. Each card sourced directly from Calon's real proposal intelligence:
STAGE 02 — During the Job £42k/yr avg MOST LEAK
"The quote took longer than it should have. Two follow-ups before they said yes."
"Engineers show up without scope. 30 minutes vanish before anyone touches a tool."
"We knew the customer would call again. We just didn't have a way to make it happen."
Margin per job: never compared to estimate
Aggregate callout — below the three cards

Total average leak across all three stages on a £1m service business: £84,000 / year. Most of it is preventable. None of it shows on the P&L.
Styling: full-width box, dark navy background, amber £ figure large and bold.

We don't deploy software. We rebuild the workflow around how your business actually works.
'rebuild' in teal italics.
Four step cards. NEW LANGUAGE — builder framing throughout:
Map the actual workflow. Not how it should work — how it does. 30–45 minutes. Founder + ops lead.
Scope the build. Fixed price agreed before anything starts. You know the number before you commit.
We build it. You approve it. 6–14 weeks depending on scope. Your team stays involved.
Your team runs it. We measure margin recovery monthly. Iterate as the business grows.
Software compatibility statement — add below the four steps

We work alongside Jobber, ServiceM8, Simpro, Commusoft, RAM Tracking, and most field service platforms. We don't replace your software. We build what it doesn't do.
Styling: small, grey, centred. One line.

Built for founder-led firms between £500k and £5m.
'£500k' and '£5m' in teal.
Two core sector cards. Then a secondary 'also serving' chip list. Hospitality stays secondary.
Engineers on site without scope. Rework eats margin. Quotes drift across two follow-ups.
JOB HANDOVER · QUOTE SYSTEM · TIME TRACKING
Multi-site, multi-engineer. Visibility breaks at scale. Timesheets inflate. Schedules slip.

Hospitality & venues · Field service · Recruitment · Property · Training · Other founder-led service businesses
Styling: grey chip/pill tags. Small. No descriptions.
Table. Label: 'Real client data. Anonymised.' Source each row from the proposals.
24–48 hrs · multi-channel · missed out of hours
Email or WhatsApp · 30 min on-site clarification
Structured doc · scope pre-confirmed · zero on-site delay
Manual · end of week · confrontation risk
Auto-verified vs GPS · real-time flags · no argument

04 | PROOF OF WORK
We don't pitch outcomes. We recover them.
'recover' in teal italics.
Aggregate stat row — BEFORE the testimonial cards

3 operational builds live · £247k+ in total margin impact identified · 0 fixed-price overruns
Note: '£247k+ total margin impact' covers both delivered and diagnosed figures combined. Label it accurately: 'across delivered systems and completed diagnostics.' This is honest and impressive.
Two testimonial cards — sourced from real proposals:
"We were losing £400 a week on timesheets and didn't know it. The platform caught it, stopped it, and now runs itself."
net year-1 saving · 4 weeks to build
Cai, Director, Volt Secure — South Wales
"Every enquiry now gets an instant reply. We stopped losing bookings to slow responses. The system paid for itself in 6 weeks."
recovered per month · 5 weeks to build
Link to /proof page. Do not expand proof further on the homepage — the proof page handles depth.

Let's find out which workflow is costing you the most.
'costing you the most' in teal italics.

30 to 45 minutes. No deck, no pitch. We map your workflow from first enquiry to repeat work, name the biggest leak, and put a £ figure on it.
You leave with a one-page diagnostic the same day — whether or not we ever work together.
✓ Live workflow map across six stages ✓ Highest-leakage workflow quantified in £ ✓ One-page recovery priority list for Monday ✓ No follow-up unless you ask
[Book the Diagnostic →]
One reply within 4 hours · No mailing list · No automated follow-up
Prefer to talk first? WhatsApp: [number] or Email: [address]
THESE ARE OUTBOUND CAMPAIGN DESTINATIONS — NOT GENERAL PAGES

Eyebrow: FOR TRADES & CONTRACTORS
Headline: Your engineers know the job. Your systems don't.
Subhead: We build the operational backbone for trades and contracting businesses — quoting, job handover, scope confirmation, time tracking, and repeat work systems. Built around how you actually run jobs.
Pain section — sourced from HeatGlow and Volt Secure proposals

Use these specific pain points. They come from real discovery sessions:
• Quotes taking 2+ hours to build manually — HeatGlow identified £6,000–£21,600/yr in lead leakage • Out-of-hours enquiries converting to zero — boiler faults happen at 9pm, response happens at 9am • Diary capacity wasted in 2-hour windows when jobs take 45 minutes — margin mix opportunity • Stagnant quoted pipeline — £3,600–£24,000/yr in already-acquired pipeline not being followed up • No single operational view — decisions made on instinct, not data

TRADES | Electrical Contractor | South Wales | Volt Secure
'Quotes used to take 24 hours. Now they're out in under 10 minutes and tracked from the moment they're sent.'
£68k annual margin impact identified · Systems live · 10 weeks to build
[Read the full case study →]

Find out where your margin is going. 30–45 minute diagnostic. No cost, no commitment.
[Book the Diagnostic →]

Eyebrow: FOR SECURITY & FM BUSINESSES
Headline: Multi-site, multi-engineer. At some point the visibility just breaks.
Subhead: We build the operational systems that keep security and FM businesses in control as they scale — engineer scheduling, timesheet verification, job visibility, compliance tracking, and the reporting that tells you which contracts are making money.
Pain section — sourced directly from Volt Secure workflow spec

• Timesheet inflation — Volt Secure was losing £20,800/year across 10 engineers. Engineers claiming hours the van GPS contradicted. Found out on payday Friday. • Quote turnaround — 24 hours per proposal. On £40k–£50k contracts, slow quotes lose jobs. • FM advisory jobs missed — 120 job sheets per week, one person reading them all manually, one batch email on Fridays. • No engineer visibility — finding out about a problem when the customer calls. • Compliance paperwork — done on paper, filed late.

SECURITY & ELECTRICAL | Volt Secure | 10 engineers | South Wales
'We were paying £400 a week for hours that were never worked. The platform caught it in real time. Engineers self-corrected once they knew the system was running.'
Cai, Director, Volt Secure
£20,800/yr timesheet saving identified · £15,200 net year-1 benefit · 4–6 weeks to build
[Read the full case study →]

Risk reduction. This is the page a 70% convinced founder reads before booking. It answers: 'What actually happens if I give these people 45 minutes?' It must make the diagnostic feel safe and the build feel real.

The diagnostic is not a sales call.
It is 30 to 45 minutes with a founder and their ops lead. We map the actual workflow — from first enquiry to repeat work. We identify the single biggest leak. We put a £ figure on it.
You leave with the Calon Operational Diagnostic — a one-page document showing your workflow map, your three biggest leaks, and a ranked recovery priority list. We send it the same day.
We do not send a proposal unless you ask for one. We do not follow up unless you want us to.
What the diagnostic produces (show a blurred sample document image here)

• Workflow map — six stages of your job lifecycle, mapped to how you actually operate • Leak ledger — your three biggest operational leaks, ranked by £ annual impact • Recovery priority — which workflow to fix first, and why • Honest assessment — whether Calon can help and what a build would involve
[Image: blurred/greyed sample diagnostic document] Caption: 'This is what you leave with.'

If we don't identify at least £20,000 in recoverable margin, we'll tell you and close the call early. No pitch. No follow-up. No charge.
Styling: green box, prominent, just above the CTA.
Phase table — what happens after the diagnostic:
Map workflow. Identify leak. Quantify in £.
Scope the build. Fixed price agreed. You approve before anything starts.
We build it. Weekly updates. You approve at each milestone.
Your team runs it. We measure recovery monthly. Iterate as needed.

Diagnostic: free.
Builds are scoped and fixed-price after the diagnostic. Depending on scope, typically £3,000–£12,000. You get the exact number before you commit to anything.
No day rates. No open-ended engagements. Fixed scope. Fixed price. Fixed timeline.

• We don't build chatbots or AI assistants as standalone products • We don't take on businesses under £400k revenue — the operational complexity isn't there yet • We don't run open-ended consulting retainers without a scoped build underneath • We don't work with businesses that aren't ready to have their workflow questioned
If that sounds like you, the diagnostic will tell us both quickly.

Commercial credibility at decision stage. A founder who is 70% convinced reads this before booking. It must show real systems, real sectors, real outcomes — and be honest about what is delivered vs diagnosed.

Systems in the wild.
Fixed bottlenecks, measured in hours and revenue — not slide-deck adjectives.
Every system on this page is real, built, and in use. Where we show a diagnostic finding rather than a delivered system, we say so.
8.1 Proof Tier Labelling — Use These Exact Labels
System built, live, in use. Outcome measured.
Use this label on: Volt Secure timesheet platform, The Cwtch booking system, Volt Secure website rebuild.
Discovery completed. Problems identified and quantified. Proposal produced.
Use this label on: HeatGlow operational audit, Volt Secure quoting system (if not yet live).
8.2 Case Study 1 — Volt Secure Timesheet Platform (TIER 1: DELIVERED)
SECURITY & ELECTRICAL | VOLT SECURE | South Wales | 10 engineers | TIER 1: DELIVERED

THE PROBLEM: Cai was losing £20,800/year to timesheet inflation across 10 engineers. Engineers claimed hours the van GPS contradicted. He found out on payday Friday, already under pressure. He often paid it rather than confronting the engineer. One week he missed a legitimate Saturday an engineer had worked and underpaid — found out the following Monday.
WHAT WE BUILT: RAM Tracking API integration + automatic GPS cross-reference engine + discrepancy dashboard + one-click resolution + Xero payroll export + full audit trail. Engineers submit timesheets via mobile. The platform cross-references against van GPS instantly. Discrepancies appear in Cai's dashboard by Tuesday — not Friday. One click to adjust, approve, or query.
THE RESULT: • £20,800/year in identified overpayment • £15,200 net year-1 benefit after platform cost • Payroll processing: 3–4 hours → under 10 minutes • Engineers self-corrected behaviour once system went live — confrontation eliminated • Investment: £4,000 build + £300/month • Payback: under 5 months from timesheet savings alone
FOUNDER: Cai, Director, Volt Secure — 'The data vs the timesheet. There's no argument to have anymore.'
8.3 Case Study 2 — The Cwtch Booking System (TIER 1: DELIVERED)
HOSPITALITY | THE CWTCH | Cardiff | TIER 1: DELIVERED

THE PROBLEM: Enquiries arriving at all hours — evenings, weekends, bank holidays. No separation between a casual enquiry, a held date, and a confirmed booking. Deposits not chased. A 20-person booking blocking a Saturday in December that a 50-person party wanted a week later. Negative Google reviews from enquiries that went unanswered.
WHAT WE BUILT: Instant response system (in The Cwtch's voice, 24/7) + three-stage booking logic (Enquiry / Held / Confirmed) + deposit tracking with automatic 48hr and 5-day chasing + booking priority dashboard (shows value of competing enquiries side by side) + full pipeline view + automated reminders + Google review trigger post-event.
THE RESULT: • 3–5 bookings recovered per month that would have been lost to slow responses • Deposit chase rate: 0% systematic → 100% automatic • 'Held' dates without deposits flagged and released automatically • Investment: £6,000 one-off + optional £149/month management • Payback: 2–3 recovered bookings (at £800–£2,500 avg) • System live in 5 weeks
FOUNDER: Antonio, Owner, The Cwtch — 'No customer gets ignored. Ever. The system answers in seconds regardless of what we're doing.'
TRADES — HEATING & BOILER | HEATGLOW | TIER 2: DIAGNOSED (Opportunity identified through operational diagnostic. System in scoping.)

THE DISCOVERY: Five interconnected bottlenecks identified during discovery — in order of revenue impact:
1. Lead leakage — enquiries arriving on Friday afternoons and weekends converting to zero 2. Out-of-hours black hole — no response, no booking option, no qualification outside business hours 3. Diary waste — 2-hour booking windows for jobs taking 45 minutes; dead capacity not redeployed 4. Stagnant quoted pipeline — on-site quotes logged, then no systematic follow-up 5. No operational view — ServiceM8, LeadDelta, Xero running separately with no unified dashboard
THE OPPORTUNITY IDENTIFIED: Conservative: £14,400/year recoverable Mid-case: £33,600/year recoverable
Breakdown: Lead recovery (£6,000–£21,600/yr) + diary efficiency (£4,800–£24,000/yr) + pipeline reactivation (£3,600–£14,400/yr)
Note: These are diagnostic findings, not delivered results. The system is currently in scoping.
Trust. Named founders. Real location. Why Calon exists. What Calon won't take on. A founder about to give access to their operations needs to know exactly who they are dealing with.

1. Alom — name, title, background. What he builds. Why he's good at it. One honest sentence about his technical approach.
2. Fabrizio — name, title, commercial background. Why this market. One sentence.
3. Why Calon exists — one short paragraph, honest and direct. Not a mission statement. A real reason.
4. What we don't take on — the five bullets from Section 7 (How It Works). Repeat them here.
5. Location — 'Built in Wales. Working with service businesses across the UK.' Add a list or map of regions where clients are based.
6. Contact — direct email, WhatsApp number. Not a contact form.

Calon was built because most operational software is designed for businesses that have already solved their workflow problems.
The businesses we work with haven't. They're running on a mix of spreadsheets, WhatsApp, and the founder's memory — and it's costing them money they don't know they're losing.
We find it. We build the system that stops it. We hand it over. Your team runs it.
That's it.

NO navigation links in the header on this page — logo only. NO footer navigation. NO links to other pages. NO email capture form. ONE action: complete the calendar booking.
This page exists only to convert a ready-to-book founder. Do not give them a reason to leave.

HEADLINE: Book the Calon Operational Diagnostic
SUBHEAD: 30 to 45 minutes. You and your ops lead. We map the workflow, name the biggest leak, put a £ figure on it.
You leave with the one-page diagnostic the same day — whether or not we work together.
WHAT YOU GET: ✓ Workflow map — six stages of your job lifecycle ✓ Leak ledger — your three biggest operational leaks, ranked by £ impact ✓ Recovery priority list ✓ Honest view of whether we can help
HOW THE 45 MINUTES RUNS: First 15 minutes: we walk through how a job moves from first call to invoice — you talk, we map. Next 20 minutes: we focus on the 2–3 stages where the biggest gaps are. Final 10 minutes: we tell you what we found and what it's costing you.
No homework. No prep. Just turn up.
GUARANTEE: If we don't identify at least £20,000 in recoverable margin, we'll close the session early. No pitch. No follow-up.

Calendly (or equivalent) embedded directly. Not a link — embedded, visible immediately without scrolling.
Duration: 45 minutes. Availability: minimum 7 days shown. Buffer: 30 min after each session.
Confirmation email triggers immediately on booking: 'Booked. Calendar invite sent. No prep needed — just 45 minutes and access to someone who knows how your jobs move through the business. You'll get the one-page diagnostic the same day.'
Text confirmation also if mobile number captured.

One reply within 4 hours · No mailing list · No automated follow-up · No proposal unless you ask
Prefer to talk first? WhatsApp: [number] | Email: [address]
We reply within 4 hours during business days.
Sticky nav — appears on every page at all scroll depths

LOGO (left) | How It Works · Proof · Who We Help · About | [Book the Diagnostic] (button, right)
Rules: • 'Book the Diagnostic' is a teal button, not a text link • Nav collapses to hamburger on mobile — button stays visible at all times • On the /diagnostic page: nav is hidden entirely. Logo only. • 'Who We Help' is a dropdown: Trades & Contractors / Security & FM

LOGO | How It Works · Proof · Who We Help · About · Book the Diagnostic
Contact: [email] | WhatsApp: [number] Location: Wales, UK — working with service businesses across the UK
NO: social media links (unless LinkedIn is active and maintained), no blog link, no AI services categories, no 'powered by' badges.
Homepage hero, How It Works, Booking page
Build this first. It makes the abstract concrete in under 5 seconds.
Homepage proof, Security page, Proof page
Real name (Cai), real company, real £ figures. Most powerful trust asset available.
Real name (Antonio), real venue. Second most powerful.
Label clearly as TIER 2: DIAGNOSED. Honest framing.
Alom + Fabrizio. Name, title, one-line background each. Non-negotiable.
Signals selectivity. Builds trust through honesty.
How It Works, Booking page, CTA sections
'If we don't find £20k+ in recoverable margin, we close early.'
'We work alongside Jobber, ServiceM8, Simpro, Commusoft...'
'No follow-up unless you ask.' Repeated consistently.
'One reply within 4 hours.' Commit and keep it.
For trades founders who won't book online. Essential.
'Built in Wales. Working with service businesses across the UK.'
One primary action across every page: Book the Diagnostic.

Not 'Get started.' Not 'Learn more.' Not 'Book a call.' 'Book the Diagnostic' — it names the specific output and sets the expectation before they arrive.
All CTAs go to /diagnostic. Not to a form. Not to an email. To the calendar.
Place a CTA block after every major section on the homepage.

After the Margin Leak Framework: 'Sound familiar? Find out what it's costing you. [Book the Diagnostic →]'
After How We Operate: 'Want to see this in action? [Book the Diagnostic →]'
After the proof section: 'Ready to find out what we'd find in your business? [Book the Diagnostic →]'
These don't need to be large — a single line with a button. But they must be there. Not every founder scrolls to the bottom.
Three conversion paths to the booking page.

Path 1: Calendar booking (primary) — Calendly embed, book directly. Path 2: WhatsApp — for founders who won't book online. Link opens WhatsApp chat. Path 3: Direct email — for founders who prefer email first.
All three must be visible on the booking page. The calendar is primary. WhatsApp and email are fallbacks that capture leads who would otherwise leave.
What happens immediately after booking — currently not specified.

On booking: 1. Redirect to a confirmation page (not just a Calendly 'thank you'). Calon-branded. Message: 'Booked. You'll get a calendar invite within the hour. No prep needed.' 2. Confirmation email with: date/time, one sentence ('Think about a job that recently didn't go as planned — that's usually where we find the biggest leak'), and a direct mobile number. 3. 24 hours before the session: one reminder. Text preferred. Email if no mobile. 4. Same day as session: the one-page diagnostic delivered by email.
14. What to Delete from the Current Site
Delete everything listed here before the new site goes live.

• Homepage hero: 'Transform Your Business with Cutting-Edge AI Solutions' — entire section • Subhead: 'AI Consultancy Designed to Cut Costs, Boost Productivity...' — entire section • 'Agentive AI: The Workforce of 1000 Employees' — entire section • All existing testimonials: Sarah (e-commerce CEO), Klaus (FreshSource), Elena (VistaTrends) — all fabricated-looking, all wrong sector • FAQ section in current form — wrong ICP, wrong questions, wrong framing • Industries page: retail, healthcare, finance, SaaS, logistics — wrong verticals entirely • All chatbot / GPT assistant case studies (PinnacleTech, HarborPoint, etc.) • '£199/month' pricing reference — wrong anchor, wrong buyer • 'Results within 48 hours' and 'ROI within 30 days' — hype claims with no substance • University partnership claim — vague, unsubstantiated • '100+ businesses' claim — not behind it • Blog — all current content. Redirect /blog to homepage. Do not leave it live. • Any page or section using the words: AI consultancy, cutting-edge AI, chatbot, prompt engineering, digital transformation, agentive AI
The visual spine of the entire site. Everything else references it. Build before writing homepage code.
Every CTA on the site sends here. It must exist before the homepage is live.
Clear the site before building. Do not build on top of old content.
Primary commercial asset. Uses the framework graphic from step 1.
Risk-reduction page. Needed for any warm lead doing due diligence before booking.
Most powerful proof asset. Publish on Proof page and Security/FM vertical page.
Second most powerful proof asset. Publish on Proof page.
Assemble all proof assets with tier labelling. Use the framework from Section 8.
Named founders, location, why Calon exists, what we don't do.
Test all CTAs, calendar embed, mobile layout, nav on all pages.
16. The Three Decisions for Today's Alom Sync
WALK INTO THE MEETING WITH THESE AGREED. EVERYTHING ELSE FOLLOWS.

Agree the final homepage section order:
Hero (with framework graphic) → Margin Leak Framework (3 stage cards) → How We Operate (4 steps) → Verticals (2 core sectors) → Before/After Table → Proof Snapshot → Final CTA
This is the recommended order. Alom may have layout constraints. Agree or adjust in the meeting — but do not leave without a finalised sequence.
Key question: Does the Operational Visibility Framework graphic go IN the hero (right side on desktop) or BELOW the hero as a standalone section?

Agree how to label the three tiers of proof on the site:
TIER 1: DELIVERED — Volt Secure timesheet, The Cwtch booking system TIER 2: DIAGNOSED — HeatGlow operational audit TIER 3: DESIGNED — BRIC Living, others
Key question: Are we comfortable naming Volt Secure (Cai) and The Cwtch (Antonio) by name? If yes, these become the headline proof assets. If not, what attribution are we using?
Second question: Do we include the HeatGlow diagnostic story as TIER 2 with honest framing, or hold it back until the system is live?

Agree the final version of the six-stage framework:
ENQUIRY → QUOTE → SCHEDULE → DELIVER → INVOICE → REPEAT
With £ leak figures at each stage sourced from the proposals.
Key question: Are the £ figures in the framework drawn from Calon's own client data, or are they illustrative based on the diagnostic intelligence from the proposals?
If illustrative: add 'typical for a £1m–£2m service business, based on diagnostic findings' as the attribution line. If from client data: use actual figures from Volt Secure and HeatGlow and attribute them.
This framework is the most important single asset in the site. Agree it completely before Alom starts building.
Calon AI Solutions — Website V2 Final Architecture — v1.0 — June 2026
Prepared by Vector Consulting. Single source of truth for the V2 build.
